summary: In hypothermic patients, axial temperature—measured in the armpit—is recommended as it provides an easily obtainable, safe, and reliable method during critical conditions like those encountered at high altitudes. This avoids risks associated with oral, rectal, or tympanic measures which can be less feasible or potentially harmful under these circumstances.
